original_abstract: This paper examines the load-bearing capacity of steel railway bridges dating back to the second half of the 19th century. The analysis is prompted by a study previously published in the quarterly journal *Roads and Bridges - Drogi i Mosty*, No. 2 (2014). We argue that when verifying the structural integrity of bridges that have been in service for over a century, it is essential to account for the long-term degradation of material properties and the cumulative effects of decades of operational fatigue.
humanized_abstract: Structural load-carrying capacity of steel railway bridges built in mid-19th century is examined. The reason for preparing this paper is the previously published article published in quarterly journal *Roads and Bridges - Drogi i Mosty*, No. 2 (2014). According to the authors, in the assessment of century-old bridges it is necessary to take into consideration the changes in the properties of the materials occurring during time and the fatigue load accumulated during many years of operation.
